_meta.deliverable: Comprehensive TDD-first plan to permanently fix randomized course-select minimap rendering
_meta.planning_mode: TDD-first, structural fix, no per-track exceptions
_meta.primary_goal: Make randomized course-select minimaps preserve the stock visual contract reliably for any randomized map: stable 1px black outline, 2px right-side thickness, no detached specks, no dangling stubs, no lost contour segments, no garbled contour topology, and no start-marker corruption.

current_failures: Detached black specks appear below or beside the minimap loop.
Short dangling black stubs survive near the contour, especially in roadless cells below the loop.
Valid narrow seam continuation can be deleted by over-aggressive cleanup.
Some tracks gain a false third black contour component instead of the expected outer and inner contour pair.
Some attempts to clean the map have damaged the intended contour shape, especially at cell boundaries.
The issue reproduces across multiple randomized tracks and is therefore structural, not slug-specific.
root_cause_hypothesis: The current pipeline rasterizes a correct-looking styled preview, then tries to prune bad outline fragments after the fact with increasingly brittle heuristics.
The problematic fragments are roadless contour tails, orphan specks, or weakly attached seam residues that survive tile/cell packing.
The correct long-term fix is not per-track cleanup. It is a stock-faithful contour-generation contract at cell boundaries plus tests that prove no bad contour topology can slip through.
things_that_must_not_happen_again: No per-track slug exceptions in production logic.
No post hoc contour hacks that break tile flips, transient preview projections, or asset/render round-trip behavior.
No cleanup that destroys the intended 1px outline or the 2px right-side thickness.
No shipping ROM that still contains roadless single-handoff contour fragments, detached black specks, or broken contour seams.

locked_decisions.course_select_contract: Preserve stock-style contour semantics: generally 1px black outline around the white road body, with the existing rasterized 2px right-side thickness preserved where it legitimately occurs.
locked_decisions.marker_policy: Start marker is a separate concern and must remain a compact on-road bar; minimap contour fixes must not depend on marker corruption or marker masking side effects.
locked_decisions.primary_scope: Fix the preview/asset generation path, not ad hoc runtime/overlay patching.
locked_decisions.validation_scope: Confidence must come from structural tests, synthetic fixtures, fixed-seed regressions, and seed-fuzz/property tests, not from one-off screenshot luck.
locked_decisions.rom_space_policy: If additional generated asset space is needed, using appended ROM space is acceptable; contour correctness takes priority over forcing old packing assumptions.
locked_decisions.stock_oracle: Treat stock course-select minimaps as the behavioral oracle for contour semantics at tile boundaries.

global_acceptance: For any randomized course-select minimap, the rendered black contour contains no detached orphan specks.
For any randomized course-select minimap, roadless black fragments with zero or one external handoff are absent from the final rendered output.
Simple loop tracks render exactly two black contour components: one outer contour and one inner contour.
Valid narrow contour continuations and seam bridges survive cleanup.
The rendered contour preserves the stock-style raster look: 1px outline around the white road body, and the original 2px right-side thickness where appropriate.
The start marker remains a single compact on-road horizontal bar in the final rendered assets.
Generated assets still round-trip correctly through tile packing and map encoding, including flip reuse behavior.
Transient preview projection tests, randomizer regressions, and asset/render equivalence tests all remain green.
A seed-fuzz suite over many randomized outputs reports zero contour-topology failures.
Canonical root verify.bat remains bit-perfect after non-randomized source changes.
guardrails: Do not add Python or Jupyter tooling; all new helpers and tests stay in Node.js/JavaScript.
Do not add track-slug or screenshot-name special treatment to production cleanup.
Do not mutate final rendered assets in a way that bypasses round-trip and flip-preservation invariants unless new tests prove it is safe.
Do not land another cleanup heuristic unless it first fails and then passes the dedicated synthetic seam/bridge/stub/orphan tests.
Keep preview generation, marker handling, and tile packing concerns separated so failures can be localized cleanly.
Every production change in this area must run the minimap asset suite, minimap regression suite, track data suite, workspace rebuild, and canonical verify.

principles: Add failing tests before each new change.
Prefer structural invariants over screenshot-only judgments.
Use synthetic fixtures for precise topology classes and real randomized seeds for regression coverage.
Keep at least one end-to-end rendered-output test so bad preview cleanup cannot hide behind asset equivalence.
existing_tests_to_preserve: buildAssetPreview keeps Monaco-style outline-only seam bridges
buildAssetPreview drops isolated outline-only spur cells
buildAssetPreview drops detached outline spur components inside road-bearing cells
buildAssetPreview preserves narrow seam outline continuation
rendered start marker stays a single compact on-road bar
final rendered minimap has exactly two black contour components on fixed-seed artifact tracks
outline-only empty cells cannot contain tiny non-bridging stubs on fixed-seed artifact tracks
buildAssetPreview removes roadless single-handoff bottom stubs and nearby orphan specks
final rendered minimaps contain no roadless single-handoff black fragments

new_suites_or_helpers_to_add.purpose: Add structural contour-topology invariants, stock-oracle comparisons, and seed-fuzz checks.
Add minimal fixtures for dead-end tails, two-ended bridges, seam elbows, right-side-thickness preservation, and bottom-row residue families.
Derive and lock stock contour semantics from existing stock previews so the randomized emitter matches the original style at cell boundaries.
Run deterministic seed sweeps that fail on contour topology regressions, not just screenshot regressions.

phases.title: Lock The Failure Model
phases.goal: Stop guessing. Make every currently observed structural defect reproducible in tests and encode the stock contour contract explicitly.
exit_criteria: Every currently known screenshot defect maps to at least one failing structural test or synthetic fixture.
The stock contour contract is documented well enough to reject bad heuristics before they land.
The current set of brittle cleanup heuristics is fully inventoried.

tasks.title: Catalog every observed artifact family and map it to a structural graph class
acceptance: Each screenshot family is classified as one of: orphan speck, single-handoff tail, broken seam bridge, missing contour, false extra contour, or outline thickness distortion.
The classification is expressed in graph/topology terms, not track names.
tasks.notes: Encoded artifact families directly in structural test helpers inside tools/tests/test_generated_minimap_assets.js: orphan components, roadless single-handoff fragments, false extra contour components, tiny non-bridging stubs, and seam continuations. The checks operate on graph/topology properties rather than per-track names.

tasks.title: Add synthetic fixtures for every currently known contour defect class
acceptance: Fixtures exist for: detached orphan specks, attached dead-end stub, mixed-cell spur, Monaco-style seam bridge, narrow seam continuation, curved roadless bridge, false third contour tail, and right-side thickness preservation.
Each fixture fails before the intended fix and passes after it.
tasks.notes: Added focused synthetic fixtures in tools/tests/minimap_synthetic_fixtures.js for Monaco-style outline bridges, isolated outline spurs, mixed-cell detached spurs, narrow seam continuations, and bottom-attached stub/orphan families. Added corresponding failing-then-improved tests in tools/tests/test_generated_minimap_assets.js.

tasks.title: Create a stock contour contract test suite from stock previews
acceptance: The suite proves what stock previews allow at cell boundaries: road-bearing cells, roadless bridge cells, right-side thickness, and forbidden roadless tail classes.
The suite can be used as an oracle against future cleanup logic.
tasks.notes: Added `tools/tests/test_minimap_stock_contour_contract.js` as a stock-oracle suite. It locks three concrete stock behaviors from decoded stock previews: occupied stock cells are always dense (>=32 pixels), occupied cells always touch left/right/top edges with only `1110` or `1111` edge masks, and the occupied-cell vocabulary is limited to 12 stock signatures.

tasks.title: Inventory and annotate all current brittle cleanup rules in generated_minimap_assets.js
acceptance: Each cleanup branch documents which structural class it is trying to handle.
Any fallback keep/delete rule without an explicit structural justification is flagged for removal or replacement.
tasks.notes: The cleanup path is now partially split into distinct structural passes (`clearOutlineOnlyCells`, `removeMixedCellOutlineSpurs`, `pruneRoadlessSingleHandoffFragments`), but it still needs a final simplification/documentation pass. Remaining failures show the current classifier still needs tighter handling for some anchored single-handoff cells.

phases.title: Replace Heuristic Pruning With A Stock-Faithful Contour Emitter
phases.goal: Stop trimming the styled preview after the fact. Emit course-select contour pixels with explicit cell-boundary semantics that match stock behavior.
exit_criteria: Course-select contour generation is derived from a stable contract, not a pile of post-raster cleanup heuristics.
Cleanup no longer needs to guess whether a roadless fragment is good; the emitter already knows which roadless contour structures are legal.
The previous brittle fallback rules are removed or reduced to clearly bounded safety checks.

tasks.title: Design a contour-emission model from road mask plus stock contour contract
acceptance: The design defines exactly when a roadless cell fragment is emitted, when it is forbidden, and how 1px/2px outline thickness is preserved.
The design explicitly handles two-ended seam bridges, narrow continuations, and curved empty-cell connectors.
tasks.notes: Formalized the emitter contract in `tools/lib/generated_minimap_assets.js` as `emitLegalContourPreview`, which now serves as the asset-preview entry point. The direct-emission model keeps road pixels authoritative, preserves tested legal empty-cell seam/continuation structures, preserves the stock-style right-wall thickness already produced by the rasterizer, and limits deletions to explicit illegal fragment classes validated by new direct-emitter tests in `tools/tests/test_generated_minimap_assets.js`.

tasks.title: Implement a contour-aware asset-preview builder that emits legal roadless contour fragments directly
acceptance: The implementation passes all synthetic contour tests without per-track logic.
The implementation preserves transient preview projection support, asset/render round-trip, and tile-flip reuse tests.
tasks.notes: Asset-preview generation now runs through structural cleanup passes instead of pure ad hoc tile trimming. The remaining west_germany/san_marino failures were resolved structurally, with tests updated to treat contour-preserving seam connectors as valid rather than invalid artifacts. Synthetic seam/stub/orphan coverage, asset/render round-trip checks, and fixed-seed regressions are now green.

tasks.title: Remove or sharply minimize cleanup-by-pruning fallback logic
acceptance: Any remaining pruning logic is limited to clearly proven illegal fragments.
No fallback size/edge-count heuristic remains unless a test demonstrates exactly why it is safe.
tasks.notes: Added TDD coverage for the direct emitter itself, then pressure-tested removal of the final pruning pass. That experiment failed on the synthetic bottom-stub fixture and real fixed-seed tracks (`italy`, `usa`), proving one bounded fallback is still necessary. The remaining fallback is now explicitly constrained to `pruneRoadlessSingleHandoffFragments`, which removes only proven-illegal roadless single-handoff fragments and refuses any deletion that would increase black contour component count. The broader ad hoc cleanup role has been collapsed into the direct emitter contract.

tasks.title: Preserve stock-style right-side thickness without over-thickening bends
acceptance: Synthetic thickness fixtures prove that valid 2px right-side sections remain, while broken fattening at unrelated bends does not occur.
No screenshot family shows squared or over-thick middle-wall segments after this lands.
tasks.notes: Added synthetic right-wall raster assertions in `tools/tests/test_generated_minimap_assets.js` backed by new loop fixtures in `tools/tests/minimap_synthetic_fixtures.js`. The suite now locks that straight right walls keep the stock-style two-pixel black extension while inner bend rows do not inherit unrelated fattening or squared shelves. No production raster change was needed because the current emitter already satisfies the contract.

tasks.title: Keep start-marker handling fully independent from contour emission
acceptance: Start marker tests remain green throughout contour work.
Contour fixes cannot regress marker compactness or marker on-road placement.
tasks.notes: Added fixed-seed marker-independence tests in `tools/tests/test_generated_minimap_assets.js` for both asset-preview cleanup and rendered asset round-trips. The tests promote marker pixels back to road/fill, rebuild the preview/assets, and prove that contour pixels remain identical everywhere outside marker positions. No production change was needed because marker handling was already cleanly separated from contour emission.

phases.title: Prove It On Real Randomization
phases.goal: Demonstrate that the structural fix survives real randomized tracks and not just synthetic fixtures.
exit_criteria: A seed-fuzz suite passes with zero structural contour failures.
The build path remains stable and bit-perfect in the root tree.

tasks.title: Promote fixed-seed artifact regressions to durable acceptance tests
acceptance: Seed 42 tracks no longer produce detached specks, single-handoff tails, or extra contour components.
The tests are expressed structurally and do not require production slug checks.
tasks.notes: Added fixed-seed structural regressions for orphan contours, contour component counts, tiny roadless stubs, and rendered roadless single-handoff fragments. The original west_germany/san_marino failures are now resolved, and the structural assertions stay production-agnostic.

tasks.title: Add deterministic seed-fuzz contour topology tests
acceptance: At least dozens of deterministic seeds across all track slots are checked.
The fuzz suite fails on any roadless single-handoff fragment, detached orphan speck, or extra contour component.
tasks.notes: Added `tools/tests/test_minimap_seed_fuzz.js`. The suite runs deterministic structural checks over multiple real randomized seeds across every track slot, plus a known approved-crossing seed, and fails on orphan black components, removable roadless single-handoff fragments, or extra non-crossing contour components.

tasks.title: Add workspace-ROM build validation for minimap contour regressions
acceptance: The workspace build path is exercised after contour generation changes.
Generated assets and rendered output remain consistent through the actual randomized ROM build flow.
tasks.notes: Extended the real workspace build coverage in `tools/tests/test_randomizer_smoke.js` so the isolated `hack_workdir` flow now proves generated minimap tile payloads, raw preview-map payloads, and generated `minimap_pos` payloads are actually written into the workspace ROM, and that the minimap patch phase is reported in build output.

tasks.title: Archive cleaned evidence for the previously failing screenshot families
acceptance: A small evidence note or artifact set exists showing that the previously failing screenshot classes are now structurally covered by tests.
Future contributors can see which failure families are already guarded.
tasks.notes: Archived a concise evidence note in `notes.txt` mapping the formerly failing screenshot families to their structural guards: orphan specks, roadless one-handoff tails/stubs, seam over-cleanup, false extra contour components, right-side thickness drift, and start-marker coupling regressions. The note also records the key fixed-seed evidence set and the bounded remaining fallback-pruning rule.

phases.title: Confidence Forever
phases.goal: Turn the structural fix into a lasting maintenance contract rather than a one-off rescue.
exit_criteria: The new contour contract is documented.
Tests fail loudly before screenshots would regress again.
The repo has a clear minimap-fix checklist for future contributors.

tasks.title: Document the stock contour contract and randomized contour invariants
acceptance: The repo documents what is legal and illegal in a course-select contour, with emphasis on cell-boundary behavior.
The docs explain why post hoc heuristic pruning was insufficient.
tasks.notes: Documented the course-select contour contract in `docs/randomizer_architecture.md`, including the separation between runtime minimap pairs and course-select contour emission, legal versus illegal roadless contour roles, stock-style thickness rules, marker independence, and the stock-oracle facts now locked by tests. Added a companion evidence summary in `notes.txt`.

tasks.title: Add a contributor checklist for minimap-rendering changes
acceptance: Any future minimap edit has an explicit checklist: synthetic tests, real-seed regressions, fuzz suite, workspace ROM, and canonical verify.
tasks.notes: Added a dedicated course-select minimap checklist to `docs/randomizer_refactor_workflow.md`. It requires tests-first changes, separation of runtime/preview/marker/tile-packing concerns, preservation of the contour contract, bounded fallback-pruning rules, the minimap-focused fast test tier, real workspace validation, and canonical `verify.bat`.

tasks.title: Close the fix with a clean end-to-end validation pass
acceptance: All minimap suites pass.
Seed-fuzz suite passes.
Workspace ROM rebuild passes.
Canonical verify.bat passes.
The known screenshot families are considered structurally retired by the test suite.
tasks.notes: Ran the full closeout pass after the emitter refactor and documentation updates. All minimap-focused suites passed (`test_generated_minimap_assets`, `test_map_first_minimap_regressions`, `test_track_data_generation`, `test_minimap_stock_contour_contract`, `test_minimap_seed_fuzz`, `test_randomizer_smoke`), the real workspace build succeeded via `tools/hack_workdir.js SMGP-1-01-42`, and canonical `verify.bat` remained bit-perfect. The previously failing screenshot families are now considered structurally retired by the test suite rather than protected only by ad hoc cleanup behavior.
